cards.definition: ability to do work — measured in joules (J) — cannot be created or destroyed
energy cannot be created or destroyed — only transferred between stores
W = Fd — force × distance — measured in joules
rate of energy transfer — P = E/t — measured in watts (W)
useful energy output ÷ total energy input × 100%
KE = ½mv²
GPE = mgh
energy stored in a stretched or compressed object
energy to raise 1 kg by 1°C — Q = mcΔT
energy to change state of 1 kg without temperature change

questions.question: What is conservation of energy?
options: A) Energy created in nuclear reactions
C) Energy cannot be created or destroyed — only transferred
D) Energy only stored in fuel
questions.explanation: Total energy in a closed system is always constant.

questions.question: What is work done?
questions.explanation: Work done = force × distance in direction of force. Joules.

questions.question: What is power?
options: C) Rate of energy transfer — P = E/t
questions.explanation: P = E/t. 1 watt = 1 joule per second.

questions.question: What is kinetic energy?
options: C) Energy of movement — KE = ½mv²
questions.explanation: KE = ½mv². Doubling speed quadruples KE (v is squared).

questions.question: What is GPE?
options: C) Energy due to height — GPE = mgh
questions.explanation: GPE = mgh. Increasing height increases GPE.

questions.question: What is efficiency?
questions.explanation: Efficiency < 100% for all real machines.

questions.question: What is specific heat capacity?
options: B) Energy to raise 1 kg by 1°C — Q = mcΔT
questions.explanation: Water SHC = 4200 J/kg°C — high, meaning it stores lots of thermal energy.

questions.question: What is specific latent heat?
options: B) Energy to raise 1 kg by 1°C
C) Energy to change state of 1 kg without temperature change
questions.explanation: Temperature constant during state changes — energy goes into breaking/forming bonds.

questions.question: What is elastic potential energy?
options: C) Energy stored in stretched or compressed objects
questions.explanation: E.g. springs, elastic bands, compressed gas.

questions.question: How are work done and energy related?
options: B) Work transfers energy — same unit (joules)
questions.explanation: Work done = energy transferred. 1 J = 1 N moving through 1 m.

questions.question: Energy cannot be created or destroyed.
questions.explanation: Conservation of energy is fundamental.

questions.question: Work done = force × time.
questions.explanation: Work done = force × DISTANCE (W = Fd).

questions.question: Doubling speed doubles KE.
questions.explanation: KE = ½mv² — speed squared → doubling speed QUADRUPLES KE.

questions.question: No machine can be 100% efficient.
questions.explanation: Energy always dissipated as heat — efficiency < 100%.

questions.question: Specific latent heat involves temperature change.
questions.explanation: No temperature change during state changes — energy goes into bonding changes.

questions.question: GPE = mgh.
questions.explanation: Mass × g × height.

questions.question: SHC is energy to raise 1 kg by 1°C.
questions.explanation: Q = mcΔT.

questions.question: Efficiency can exceed 100%.
questions.explanation: Would violate conservation of energy.

questions.question: Elastic potential energy is stored in deformed elastic objects.
questions.explanation: Released when object returns to original shape.

questions.question: P = E/t.
questions.explanation: Power = energy transferred per second. Watts.

questions.question: ________ of energy: cannot be created or destroyed — only transferred.
questions.explanation: Total energy in a closed system is constant.

questions.question: Work done = ________ × distance.
questions.explanation: W = Fd. Joules.

questions.question: P = E/________ — rate of energy transfer — watts.
questions.explanation: 1 watt = 1 joule per second.

questions.question: KE = ½m________² — energy of motion.
questions.explanation: Doubling speed quadruples KE.

questions.question: GPE = m × g × ________.
questions.explanation: On Earth g ≈ 10 N/kg.

questions.question: ________ = useful output ÷ total input × 100%.
questions.explanation: All real machines < 100% efficient.

questions.question: Specific ________ capacity: energy to raise 1 kg by 1°C.
questions.explanation: Q = mcΔT. Water = 4200 J/kg°C.

questions.question: Specific ________ heat: energy to change state — no temperature change.
questions.explanation: Latent heat of fusion (melting) and vaporisation (boiling).

questions.question: ________ potential energy stored in stretched or compressed objects.
questions.explanation: Springs, elastic bands, compressed air.

questions.question: Work done and energy both measured in ________.
questions.explanation: 1 J = 1 N moving through 1 m.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

pairs.match: P = E/t — rate of energy transfer — watts

cards.definition: the single force that has the same effect as all individual forces combined
no resultant force → constant velocity or rest
F = ma — resultant force = mass × acceleration
equal and opposite force pairs — act on different objects
the gravitational force on an object — W = mg
a contact force opposing relative motion — produces heat
when drag = driving force — no resultant force — constant velocity
p = mv — mass × velocity — vector quantity
in a closed system, total momentum before = total momentum after a collision
thinking distance + braking distance — affected by speed, road conditions, reaction time

questions.question: What is a resultant force?
options: B) The single force equivalent to all forces combined
C) The force of friction only
questions.explanation: The resultant (net) force determines the acceleration — if zero, object stays at rest or constant velocity.

questions.question: What is terminal velocity?
options: A) Maximum possible speed in a vacuum
B) When drag = driving force — no resultant force — constant velocity
C) The speed just before crashing
D) Speed when friction is zero
questions.explanation: Falling object: initially accelerates, drag increases, eventually drag = weight → no resultant force → terminal velocity.

questions.question: What is momentum?
questions.explanation: Momentum (p) = mass × velocity. Vector quantity — has direction as well as magnitude.

questions.question: What is conservation of momentum?
options: A) Momentum always increases in collisions
B) Total momentum before = total momentum after in a closed system
C) Only applies to elastic collisions
D) Momentum is lost in every collision
questions.explanation: In a closed system (no external forces), total momentum is conserved — before equals after.

questions.question: What is weight?
options: A) The mass of an object
B) The amount of matter in an object
C) Gravitational force on an object — W = mg
questions.explanation: Weight = mass × gravitational field strength. W = mg. On Earth, g ≈ 10 N/kg.

questions.question: What is Newton's Second Law?
options: C) F = ma — resultant force = mass × acceleration
questions.explanation: F = ma. A greater force produces greater acceleration for the same mass.

questions.question: What affects stopping distance?
options: B) Speed, road conditions (friction), tyre condition, reaction time
questions.explanation: Stopping distance = thinking distance (reaction time) + braking distance (kinetic energy, friction).

questions.question: What is Newton's Third Law?
options: B) No resultant force → constant velocity
C) Equal and opposite force pairs on different objects
questions.explanation: Newton's Third Law pairs: equal magnitude, opposite direction, same type, on DIFFERENT objects.

questions.question: What is friction?
options: A) A force in the direction of motion
B) Contact force opposing relative motion — produces heat
questions.explanation: Friction acts between surfaces in contact — it opposes motion and converts kinetic energy to heat.

questions.question: What is Newton's First Law?
options: C) No resultant force → object stays at rest or moves at constant velocity
questions.explanation: A force is needed to CHANGE motion — not to maintain it.

questions.question: Resultant force determines acceleration.
questions.explanation: F = ma — the net force produces acceleration.

questions.question: Terminal velocity is when the object is moving fastest possible.
questions.explanation: Terminal velocity is when drag = driving force — no resultant force — constant velocity.

questions.question: Momentum = mass × velocity.
questions.explanation: p = mv. Vector quantity.

questions.question: Momentum is always lost in collisions.
questions.explanation: Total momentum is CONSERVED in a closed system.

questions.question: Weight and mass are the same quantity.
questions.explanation: Mass is amount of matter (kg). Weight is gravitational force (N). W = mg.

questions.question: Newton's Third Law pairs act on the same object.
questions.explanation: Newton's Third Law pairs act on DIFFERENT objects.

questions.question: Stopping distance only depends on speed.
questions.explanation: Stopping distance also depends on reaction time, road conditions, tyre and brake condition.

questions.question: F = ma.
questions.explanation: Newton's Second Law.

questions.question: Friction always acts in the direction of motion.
questions.explanation: Friction acts AGAINST motion.

questions.question: No resultant force means the object must be stationary.
questions.explanation: No resultant force means constant velocity OR rest — could be moving.

questions.question: ________ force is the single force equivalent to all forces combined.
questions.explanation: Determines acceleration via F = ma.

questions.question: Newton's ________ Law: F = ma.
questions.explanation: Greater force → greater acceleration.

questions.question: Newton's ________ Law: no resultant force → constant velocity or rest.
questions.explanation: Force needed to CHANGE motion, not maintain it.

questions.question: Newton's ________ Law: equal and opposite pairs — on different objects.
questions.explanation: Equal magnitude, opposite direction, same type.

questions.question: ________ = mg — gravitational force on an object.
questions.explanation: On Earth g ≈ 10 N/kg.

questions.question: ________ velocity: drag = driving force — constant speed.
questions.explanation: No resultant force → no acceleration.

questions.question: ________ = mv — mass × velocity — conserved in closed systems.
questions.explanation: Vector quantity — has direction.

questions.question: Conservation of ________: total before = total after in closed system.
questions.explanation: No external forces in a closed system.

questions.question: ________ is a contact force opposing motion — produces heat.
questions.explanation: Acts between surfaces in contact.

questions.question: ________ distance = thinking distance + braking distance.
questions.explanation: Affected by speed, reaction time, road and tyre condition.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

pairs.match: no resultant force → constant velocity or rest
equal and opposite pairs — different objects
p = mv — conserved in closed systems

cards.definition: flow of charge — I = Q/t — measured in amperes (A)
work done per unit charge — V = W/Q — measured in volts (V)
opposition to current — R = V/I — measured in ohms (Ω)
V = IR — for ohmic conductors at constant temperature
same current, voltages add up
same voltage across branches, currents add up
EM waves ordered by frequency — radio → microwave → infrared → visible → UV → X-ray → gamma
v = fλ — frequency × wavelength
vibrations perpendicular to direction of travel — all EM waves
vibrations parallel to direction of travel — sound

questions.question: What is Ohm's Law?
questions.explanation: Ohmic conductor: V proportional to I at constant temperature.

questions.question: What is potential difference?
options: B) Work done per unit charge — volts
questions.explanation: V = W/Q. Drives current around the circuit.

questions.question: Series circuit — what is the same throughout?
questions.explanation: Series: same current everywhere. Voltages add up.

questions.question: Parallel circuit — what is the same across each branch?
questions.explanation: Parallel: same voltage across each branch. Currents add up.

questions.question: What is the wave speed equation?
questions.explanation: v = fλ. Speed in m/s, frequency in Hz, wavelength in m.

questions.question: What is a transverse wave?
options: B) Vibrations perpendicular to travel — e.g. light
questions.explanation: Transverse: perpendicular vibrations. All EM waves are transverse.

questions.question: What is a longitudinal wave?
options: B) Vibrations parallel to travel — compressions and rarefactions — e.g. sound
questions.explanation: Sound is longitudinal — compressions and rarefactions travel in the same direction as the wave.

questions.question: What is resistance?
options: C) Opposition to current flow — R = V/I — ohms
questions.explanation: Higher resistance → less current for same voltage.

questions.question: What is the EM spectrum ordered by?
options: C) Frequency (and wavelength) — radio lowest, gamma highest frequency
questions.explanation: Radio: lowest frequency, longest wavelength. Gamma: highest frequency, shortest wavelength.

questions.question: What is current?
options: B) Flow of charge — I = Q/t — amperes
questions.explanation: Current = charge flow per second. I = Q/t.

questions.question: V = IR.
questions.explanation: Ohm's Law — for ohmic conductors at constant temperature.

questions.question: In a series circuit, current varies between components.
questions.explanation: Series: SAME current throughout.

questions.question: In a parallel circuit, voltage differs between branches.
questions.explanation: Parallel: SAME voltage across each branch.

questions.question: All EM waves travel at the same speed in a vacuum.
questions.explanation: 3 × 10⁸ m/s in a vacuum.

questions.question: v = fλ.
questions.explanation: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength.

questions.question: Sound is a transverse wave.
questions.explanation: Sound is LONGITUDINAL — vibrations parallel to direction of travel.

questions.question: Resistance is measured in volts.
questions.explanation: Resistance in OHMS (Ω). Voltage in volts (V).

questions.question: Gamma rays have the lowest frequency in the EM spectrum.
questions.explanation: Gamma has the HIGHEST frequency.

questions.question: P = IV.
questions.explanation: Power = current × voltage. Also P = I²R.

questions.question: Current = charge ÷ time.
questions.explanation: I = Q/t. Measured in amperes.

questions.question: ________ = V/I — opposition to current — ohms.
questions.explanation: Higher resistance → less current.

questions.question: Ohm's Law: V = ________.
questions.explanation: For ohmic conductors at constant temperature.

questions.question: Series: same ________ — voltages add up.
questions.explanation: Total voltage = sum of component voltages.

questions.question: Parallel: same ________ across branches — currents add up.
questions.explanation: Total current = sum of branch currents.

questions.question: v = f × ________.
questions.explanation: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength.

questions.question: ________ waves: perpendicular vibrations — all EM waves.
questions.explanation: Water waves, light, all EM radiation.

questions.question: ________ waves: parallel vibrations — e.g. sound.
questions.explanation: Compressions and rarefactions.

questions.question: EM spectrum: radio → microwave → IR → visible → UV → ________ → gamma.
questions.explanation: Increasing frequency and energy.

questions.question: Current = ________ ÷ time.
questions.explanation: I = Q/t. Measured in amperes.

questions.question: P = I × ________.
questions.explanation: Power = current × voltage.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

pairs.match: radio → micro → IR → visible → UV → X-ray → gamma

blocks.block_name: Separate Science: Moments, Space & Particle Physics

cards.definition: turning effect of a force — moment = force × perpendicular distance from pivot
for balance: clockwise moments = anticlockwise moments
P = F/A — force per unit area — measured in pascals (Pa)
P = ρgh — increases with depth
upward force on submerged object = weight of fluid displaced
splitting of a heavy nucleus — releases large amounts of energy — used in nuclear power
joining of light nuclei — releases even more energy — occurs in stars
time for half the radioactive nuclei to decay — constant for each isotope
light from distant galaxies shifted to longer wavelengths — evidence Universe is expanding
Universe began as an extremely dense, hot point — has been expanding ever since

questions.question: What is a moment?
options: B) Turning effect of a force — force × perpendicular distance from pivot
questions.explanation: Moment (N⋅m) = force (N) × perpendicular distance from pivot (m).

questions.question: What is the principle of moments?
options: B) Clockwise moments = anticlockwise moments for balance
C) Centre of mass at pivot
D) Forces through centre of mass
questions.explanation: Rotational equilibrium: Σ clockwise moments = Σ anticlockwise moments.

questions.question: What is nuclear fission?
options: C) Splitting a heavy nucleus — releases large amounts of energy
questions.explanation: Fission: heavy nucleus (U-235) splits → smaller nuclei + neutrons + energy. Used in nuclear reactors.

questions.question: What is nuclear fusion?
options: C) Joining light nuclei — releases very large amounts of energy — occurs in stars
questions.explanation: Fusion: light nuclei (hydrogen isotopes) join → helium + energy. Powers stars. Requires extreme temperature and pressure.

questions.question: What is half-life?
options: A) Time for all radioactive atoms to decay
B) Time for half the radioactive nuclei to decay — constant for each isotope
C) The rate of nuclear fission
D) Energy released in one decay
questions.explanation: Half-life is constant and unique to each isotope — used for carbon dating and nuclear medicine.

questions.question: What does red-shift tell us?
options: B) Light from distant galaxies shifted to longer wavelengths — evidence Universe is expanding
questions.explanation: The further the galaxy, the greater the red-shift — all galaxies moving away supports the Big Bang.

questions.question: What is pressure?
options: B) Force per unit area — P = F/A — pascals
questions.explanation: P = F/A. 1 pascal = 1 N/m². Pressure increases with force, decreases with area.

questions.question: What is upthrust?
options: A) Downward force on submerged object
B) Upward force = weight of fluid displaced
questions.explanation: Archimedes' principle: upthrust = weight of fluid displaced. If upthrust > weight, object floats.

questions.question: What is liquid pressure?
options: C) P = ρgh — increases with depth and density
questions.explanation: Liquid pressure: ρ = density, g = gravitational field strength, h = depth.

questions.question: What is the Big Bang theory?
options: B) Universe began as hot dense point — has been expanding ever since
questions.explanation: Supported by red-shift and cosmic microwave background radiation (CMBR).

questions.question: Moment = force × perpendicular distance from pivot.
questions.explanation: Units: N⋅m.

questions.question: For balance, clockwise moments exceed anticlockwise.
questions.explanation: For BALANCE: clockwise = anticlockwise moments.

questions.question: Nuclear fission releases energy by joining nuclei.
questions.explanation: FISSION splits nuclei. FUSION joins nuclei.

questions.question: Nuclear fusion powers stars.
questions.explanation: Stars fuse hydrogen to helium — releasing enormous energy.

questions.question: Half-life varies with temperature and pressure.
questions.explanation: Half-life is constant — unaffected by temperature or pressure.

questions.question: Red-shift provides evidence the Universe is expanding.
questions.explanation: Galaxies moving away → light waves stretched → longer wavelength.

questions.question: Liquid pressure decreases with depth.
questions.explanation: Liquid pressure INCREASES with depth (P = ρgh).

questions.question: Upthrust is a downward force.
questions.explanation: Upthrust is UPWARD — equal to weight of fluid displaced.

questions.question: P = F/A.
questions.explanation: Pressure = force ÷ area. Pascals.

questions.question: Big Bang theory is supported by CMBR and red-shift.
questions.explanation: Both provide independent evidence for the Big Bang.

questions.question: ________ = force × perpendicular distance from pivot — turning effect.
questions.explanation: Units: N⋅m.

questions.question: Principle of moments: ________ moments = anticlockwise for balance.
questions.explanation: Σ clockwise = Σ anticlockwise.

questions.question: P = F/________ — force per unit area — pascals.
questions.explanation: Pressure increases with force, decreases with area.

questions.question: Liquid pressure P = ρg________ — increases with depth.
questions.explanation: ρ = density, g = gravitational field strength.

questions.question: ________ is upward force on submerged object = weight of fluid displaced.
questions.explanation: Archimedes' principle.

questions.question: Nuclear ________ splits heavy nuclei — used in power stations.
questions.explanation: U-235 splits → smaller nuclei + neutrons + energy.

questions.question: Nuclear ________ joins light nuclei — powers stars.
questions.explanation: Requires extreme temperature and pressure.

questions.question: ________ is the time for half the radioactive nuclei to decay.
questions.explanation: Constant for each isotope — used in carbon dating.

questions.question: ________ shows galaxies moving away — evidence for Universe expanding.
questions.explanation: Greater distance = greater red-shift.

questions.question: ________ Bang: Universe from hot dense point — expanding ever since.
questions.explanation: Evidence: red-shift and cosmic microwave background.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

pairs.match: splits heavy nucleus — energy released — power stations
joins light nuclei — enormous energy — powers stars
